seoTitle: How to Connect Snowflake to Power BI Directly
metaDescription: Learn how to set up a direct connection between Snowflake and Power BI, so you can build reports on top of your cloud data warehouse quickly.
intro: Snowflake has become a common home for company data, and getting Power BI connected to it directly opens up real-time analysis on top of that warehouse. This guide walks through setting up a direct connection between the two platforms. It's aimed at data analysts and BI professionals who want to build dashboards straight from Snowflake without extra middle steps.

steps.heading: Set up Snowflake for the connection
steps.body: Make sure you have the correct account identifier, warehouse, and database details ready, along with credentials that have permission to read the relevant data.

steps.heading: Connect to Power BI
steps.body: In Power BI Desktop, use Get Data and select the Snowflake connector, entering your server and warehouse information to begin authentication.

steps.heading: Finalize the connection
steps.body: Choose between Import or DirectQuery mode depending on your reporting needs, then select the tables or views you want to bring into your model.

steps.heading: Review use cases and benefits
steps.body: Apply the connection to scenarios like building near real-time dashboards on top of large Snowflake datasets without needing to duplicate data elsewhere.

faq.question: Should I use Import or DirectQuery mode when connecting Snowflake to Power BI?
faq.answer: Import mode is often faster for report interaction but requires refreshes to stay current, while DirectQuery keeps data live at the cost of some performance, so the choice depends on your priorities.

faq.question: What credentials does Power BI need to connect to Snowflake?
faq.answer: You'll typically need your Snowflake account URL along with a username and password, or single sign-on credentials if your organization has that configured.

faq.question: Can I connect to multiple Snowflake databases in one Power BI report?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you have access, you can bring in tables from multiple databases or schemas within the same Snowflake account into a single report.

faq.question: Does this connection support automatic scheduled refreshes?
faq.answer: Yes, if you're using Import mode, you can configure scheduled refreshes through the Power BI service to keep the imported data current.
